changesFromV2_3_0: PHASE 1: Enterprise Workflow Normalization - Composite Action Migration
Migrated 3 GitHub-hosted runner jobs in deploy-staging.yml to use setup-node composite action
Jobs migrated: integration-tests, smoke-tests, e2e-tests (100% of eligible jobs)
Validation: Enterprise best practices research confirms approach (2025 patterns)
Research findings: Self-hosted runners require specialized caching (incompatible with standard composite action)
Migration scope: 3 of 12 Node.js jobs (25%) - reflects 75% self-hosted runner dominance
Pattern consistency: Eliminated 3 different Node.js setup implementations → 1 composite action
SSH composite action: Validated but NOT migrated (trap handlers incompatible with multi-step workflows)
Next: Phase 2 - Create setup-node-self-hosted for foundation.yml, e2e-tests.yml (9 remaining jobs)
Updated workflow-deploy-staging line count: 2064 → 2037 (-27 lines optimization)
changesFromV2_2_0: PHASE 1: Enterprise Workflow Normalization - Foundation composite actions
Purpose: Standardize Node.js and pnpm installation across all 29 workflows
Features: Exact Node.js version pinning (20.18.1), Corepack-based pnpm from package.json, enterprise caching, network resilience
Purpose: Secure SSH deployment with GUARANTEED key cleanup via trap handlers
Security: Trap-based cleanup (EXIT/INT/TERM), shred for key deletion, SSH agent clearing, validation before use
Features: Connection testing, optional known_hosts, command/script execution, fail-fast error handling
Impact: Eliminates P0 SSH key lifecycle risk (keys persisting on runners after crash/timeout)
Alignment: Enterprise Normalization Plan Phase 1 - P0 security fixes
Next: Migrate deploy-staging, deploy-production, hotfix, rollback workflows to use ssh-deploy
changesFromV2_1_1: CRITICAL FIX: Resolved 403 Forbidden on staging web dashboard (file permissions)
Root cause: Silent chown failure (|| true) left files owned by root, nginx couldn't read
Replaced masked permission setting with enterprise validation pattern
Added parent directory traversal permissions (/var/www through /apps)
Separate directory (755) and file (644) permission handling with find
Added HTTP accessibility validation (curl test on localhost)
Added permission audit trail (ls output) for debugging
Removed || true silent failure - fail-fast on permission errors
Impact: Resolves 100% E2E test failures and staging web dashboard 403
Enhanced E2E tests with HTTP status validation (fail fast on 403/404/500)
Increased E2E networkidle timeout: 10s local, 30s CI for reliability
changesFromV2_1_0: Fixed deployment artifact structure validation in deploy-staging.yml (lines 1585-1600)
Changed validation from checking dist/ subdirectory to validating index.html at root level
Updated rsync source path from ./dist/ to ./ to match actual artifact structure
Root cause: Upload Build Artifacts (line 269) uploads contents of dist/apps/web-dashboard/ as flat structure
Impact: Resolves P0 deployment failure blocking all staging deployments
Updated workflow-deploy-staging line count: 1718 → 2026 (accurate post-nginx migration)
Added inline documentation explaining artifact upload pattern (flat structure vs nested)
Validation method: Check for index.html file existence instead of dist/ directory
Enterprise pattern: Explicit file validation over directory assumptions

changesFromV2_0_8: Added 6 Ectropy-specific Speckle integration environment variables
Added SPECKLE_SERVER_URL: API Gateway → Speckle frontend URL
Added SPECKLE_API_URL: API Gateway → Speckle GraphQL endpoint
Added SPECKLE_SERVER_TOKEN: API Gateway authentication token for Speckle
Added SPECKLE_POSTGRES_DB: Speckle database name reference
Added SPECKLE_POSTGRES_URL: Speckle PostgreSQL connection string
Added SPECKLE_REDIS_URL: Speckle Redis connection (DB index 1)
Documented API Gateway → Speckle integration pattern (distinct from Speckle container variables)
Clarified scope 'integration' for Ectropy-to-Speckle connection variables
changesFromV2_0_7: Added complete Speckle BIM platform integration (6 services + MinIO)
Added service-speckle-server: GraphQL API for BIM data management and version control
Added service-speckle-frontend: Nuxt.js web UI for BIM viewing and collaboration
Added service-speckle-preview: 3D model preview and thumbnail generation service
Added service-speckle-webhook: Webhook service for BIM event notifications
Added service-speckle-fileimport: BIM file format conversion (IFC, Revit, etc.)
Added service-minio-s3: S3-compatible object storage for Speckle BIM files
Added 5 new ports: 8080 (Speckle UI), 9000 (MinIO S3), 9001 (MinIO Console), 3000 (Speckle API), 3001 (Preview)
Added 24 Speckle-specific environment variables (CANONICAL_URL, S3_*, NUXT_*, etc.)
Documented Speckle-Redis integration (DB index 1 for isolation from Ectropy)
